in a case study, a patient with autism underwent chelation therapy to improve symptoms. the treatment was administered under the supervision of a licensed medical professional and resulted in reduced aggression and improved communication. the patient’s progress was monitored closely and the therapy was discontinued after six months. the treatment was not a standard medical procedure and was not approved by the medical community. the information is presented as a research finding and not a medical recommendation.

the case study highlights the potential benefits of chelation therapy for autism. the patient showed significant improvement in social interaction and language development. the treatment was not a standard medical procedure and was not approved by the medical community. the information is presented as a research finding and not a medical recommendation.
